name: BROSIER/CUNNINGHAM RES
address: 2441 NW SKYLINE RANCH RD, BEND, OR 97703

fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operations at the property could be achieved by addressing the limited rear access and dense vegetation. Improving access routes to the rear of the property, perhaps by creating a dedicated pathway or clearing obstructions, may facilitate more efficient movement for emergency personnel. In addition, managing the dense vegetation, especially close to driveways or structures, could open up space for emergency vehicle maneuvering and improve visibility around the property. Maintaining clear sightlines and unobstructed access throughout the landscaped areas and around any fencing could further enhance staging and navigation opportunities, supporting effective emergency response activities.

wildfire.recommendations: To enhance wildfire preparedness, increasing the separation between the dense tree canopy and the structure could improve defensible space and reduce potential fire exposure. It may also be beneficial to minimize vegetation concentrations immediately adjacent to the structure, which can decrease fuel continuity and the likelihood of fire spread toward the building. Managing the vegetation along the perimeter of the property and reducing the density of scattered trees could further limit opportunities for fire spread. Additionally, ensuring clear access routes with managed vegetation could facilitate easier firefighter access and support suppression efforts. Providing and maintaining open spaces around the structure and along the access route may contribute to long-term vegetation management and property resilience.
